Home / Blog / Your First Online Holistic Session
Holistic Wellness April 29, 2026 9 min read

Your First Online Holistic Session: A Practical Guide

Booking your first online holistic session can feel uncertain: how does it actually work over video, how do you prepare, what should you expect, and how do you avoid wasting money on the wrong practitioner? This guide answers all of it — clearly, without mystical fluff.

Editorial note — Article written by the Holistic Unity editorial team. Updated April 29, 2026. Informational content; it does not replace professional medical or psychological advice. The references on duration, cost ranges, and online practice come from current Italian market data and the official sources listed at the end of the article.

What is an online holistic session?

An online holistic session is an individual appointment with a holistic practitioner that takes place via video call. The practitioner uses the same techniques they would use in person — Reiki, ThetaHealing®, Numerology, Astrology, Human Design, holistic counselling, Family Constellation in individual format — adapted to a remote setting.

The format has grown rapidly since 2020. The advantage is concrete: you choose the practitioner that genuinely fits your needs, regardless of geography, you save travel time, and the session takes place in an environment you already know. The downside is also real: requires a stable connection, a quiet space, and a willingness to engage in front of a screen — which doesn't suit everyone.

Important clarification: a holistic session is not therapy in the medical or clinical-psychological sense. The practitioner does not diagnose, does not prescribe, and does not treat illness. It is support for personal wellbeing alongside any medical care, never instead of it.

Which disciplines work online (and which don't)

Not all holistic disciplines translate equally well to video. Here is an honest mapping of what works very well, what works with caveats, and what is better experienced in person.

Disciplines that work very well online

  • ThetaHealing®: the technique is based on guided meditation and verbal dialogue. Online sessions are the standard for many international practitioners.
  • Distance Reiki: Reiki traditionally provides for symbols of distance treatment. The client lies down comfortably while the practitioner channels energy and shares feedback at the end of the session.
  • Numerology, Astrology, Human Design: these are interpretive disciplines. The practitioner reads your birth data and discusses what emerges. The mode of delivery makes no difference.
  • Holistic counselling: based on dialogue and listening. Works as well as in person, often better because the client is in their own environment.
  • Individual Family Constellation: when conducted with figurines or representative objects on the table, it can be done online. Group constellations remain better in person.

Disciplines that require an in-person component

Treatments that involve physical contact (massage, craniosacral, shiatsu, energy work with hands-on touch), Ayurvedic abhyanga, and the practical part of Naturopathic check-ups that require physical observation are exceptions: they need a face-to-face appointment. The online consultation can serve as a follow-up but not as the main session.

How to prepare: space, time, intention

The quality of an online session depends 30% on the practitioner, 30% on the technique, and 40% on how you prepare. This is not an exaggeration — practitioners with online experience repeat it constantly.

Checklist for the day before

  • Identify a quiet space where you will not be interrupted for the duration of the session plus 15 minutes before and after.
  • Test camera, microphone, and the chosen platform with a friend the day before. The first technical issues should not happen during the session.
  • Write down 2-3 specific questions or themes you want to address. A vague intention produces a vague session.
  • Have a glass of water, a notebook, and a pen at hand.

On the day, avoid heavy meals in the previous 2 hours. Reduce caffeine if you tend to feel agitated. Silence phone notifications and close all unnecessary browser tabs. If you live with others, let them know you will not be available — even adults often forget that 'I'm in a session' means 'do not knock.'

What happens during the session: step by step

The structure varies by discipline, but a first online session generally follows this pattern:

  1. Initial conversation (10-15 minutes): the practitioner asks why you have come, what you would like to address, and any relevant context. This is also the moment to ask about consent for any recording, the format of the session, and how follow-up will work.
  2. Core technique (30-50 minutes): this is where the discipline is actually applied — guided meditation in ThetaHealing®, distance energy transmission in Reiki, reading of charts in Astrology or Human Design, individual constellation work, and so on. You may be asked to close your eyes, breathe deeply, lie down, or simply stay seated and listen.
  3. Sharing and integration (10-15 minutes): the practitioner reports what emerged, gives concrete recommendations or practical exercises for the days that follow, and answers your questions. Take notes here — much will fade within 24 hours if you don't write it down.

Total duration: between 60 and 90 minutes for the first session. Subsequent sessions tend to settle around 60 minutes.

Find a verified online holistic practitioner

On Holistic Unity all practitioners are checked: training, certifications, and reviews from real clients. Browse profiles, read methods, book the practitioner that fits your needs.

Explore practitioners

After the session: integration and honesty with yourself

An online holistic session is not a magic event. The real value emerges in the days that follow, when you observe whether the conversation, the technique, or the recommendations have produced any concrete changes in attention, mood, or behaviour.

In the 24-48 hours following the session, it is normal to feel: more tired than usual, mentally clearer on a specific topic, emotionally moved, or — in some cases — neutral. None of these reactions is a sign that the session 'worked' or 'didn't work.' Wait at least a week before drawing conclusions.

If the practitioner suggested a practical exercise (a breathing technique, a self-observation prompt, a small daily ritual), do it for at least 7 days before deciding it is useful or not. Without consistent application, no holistic discipline produces measurable effects.

How much does an online holistic session cost?

In Italy, the price of an individual online session typically falls in this range:

  • Brief readings (Numerology, focused Astrology): 40-70 euros for 45-60 minutes.
  • Standard sessions (Reiki, ThetaHealing®, holistic counselling, Human Design): 70-130 euros for 60-90 minutes.
  • In-depth or specialised sessions (individual Family Constellation, full Astrology consultation): 100-180 euros for 90-120 minutes.

Multi-session packages often offer a discount of 10-20%. Be wary of prices significantly below the market: a holistic practitioner with serious training and ongoing supervision has structural costs that prevent them from charging 20 euros per session sustainably. Excessively low prices are usually a signal of insufficient training or a hobby-level practice.

In Italy, the holistic practitioner is classified among unregulated professions under Law 4/2013. The fee is therefore subject to VAT (with the relevant tax regime) and must be reflected on a regular invoice or receipt. Always ask for a tax document — a serious practitioner provides one without hesitation.

Privacy, recordings, and your rights

An online holistic session involves the processing of personal data — sometimes sensitive: birth data, life events, emotional themes, sometimes information about your health. The European GDPR and Italian Privacy Code apply fully.

What you should expect from a serious practitioner:

  • A privacy policy available before booking, explaining what data is collected, how it is stored, and for how long.
  • Explicit consent for any audio or video recording. Recording without consent is unlawful, regardless of who initiates it.
  • Confidentiality: what you share in the session is confidential. The practitioner cannot share names, themes, or details with anyone else, even within their own training community.
  • The right to erasure: you can request the deletion of any notes, recordings, or data the practitioner holds about you, at any time, without giving a reason.

How to choose the right practitioner online

The online market has lowered the barrier to entry for practitioners. The good news: more options, fairer prices, less geographical luck. The bad news: more confusion and a higher proportion of insufficiently trained operators. Three filters help separate signal from noise.

  1. Documented training. The website or profile should state where the practitioner trained, with whom, and for how many hours. Generic phrases like 'years of personal study' are not enough. For ThetaHealing® look for THInK certification; for Reiki, the lineage of the master who initiated them; for disciplines covered by UNI 11713, registration with a recognised professional association (SIAF, AIPO, CSEN Benessere).
  2. Honest description of the method. A serious practitioner explains what they do, but also what they do not do. They write 'this discipline does not replace medical care' and 'I do not treat clinical conditions.' They do not promise specific outcomes. They do not use phrases like 'guaranteed healing,' 'definitive cure,' or 'unblocks any trauma.'
  3. Verified reviews. A few specific reviews from real clients are worth more than hundreds of generic ones. Marketplaces with verified reviews (where the reviewer must have actually booked a session) are more reliable than testimonials manually copied onto a personal site.

A useful additional step: many practitioners offer a free 15-minute introductory call. Use it. In a quarter of an hour you understand whether the communication style works for you, whether your questions are taken seriously, and whether the person on the other side seems professionally serious. It costs nothing and saves you a wrong session.

Sources and references

  • Italian law on unregulated professions: Law 14 January 2013, no. 4 (“Provisions on non-organised professions”) — official text on Gazzetta Ufficiale. Frames the legal status of online holistic practitioners in Italy.
  • UNI 11713 standard: “Non-regulated professional activities — Holistic operator” — published by Ente Italiano di Normazione (UNI), available at store.uni.com. Defines the requirements for a holistic operator's training.
  • European General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR): Regulation (EU) 2016/679 — reference text via the Italian Data Protection Authority at garanteprivacy.it. Governs the lawfulness of recordings, data retention, and the right to erasure in online sessions.
  • WHO Global Strategy for Traditional and Complementary Medicine 2014-2023: framework on the role of complementary medicine alongside conventional medicine — who.int.
  • Italian Revenue Agency (regime forfettario): reference page on the tax regime applicable to holistic practitioners with VAT registration — agenziaentrate.gov.it. Source for billing and tax requirements mentioned in the section on price.

Last reviewed: April 29, 2026. The Holistic Unity editorial team verifies links and regulatory references at each substantive update of the article.

Frequently asked

How long does an online holistic session last?

Most online holistic sessions last between 50 and 90 minutes. A first introductory session can take up to 90 minutes because it includes the initial conversation, while subsequent sessions often settle around 60 minutes. Brief readings (Numerology, focused Astrology) can last 45 minutes. Always check the duration indicated by the practitioner at booking.

What technology do I need for an online holistic session?

You need very little: a stable internet connection, a computer, tablet, or smartphone with camera and microphone, and a video-call platform (Zoom, Google Meet, WhatsApp video, or the marketplace's dedicated platform). Headphones are recommended for privacy and audio quality. No complex software or special equipment is needed.

Are online holistic sessions as effective as in-person ones?

For many disciplines, yes — and often neither option is objectively superior. ThetaHealing®, distance Reiki, Numerology, Astrology, Human Design, and dialogue-based holistic counselling work very well online. Direct bodywork, some group constellations, and treatments requiring physical contact remain better in person. The real differentiator is the practitioner's quality, not the medium.

Can I record an online holistic session?

Only with the practitioner's explicit consent, to be requested before the session begins. Many practitioners authorise an audio recording for personal use (useful for re-listening to readings or specific messages); others prefer to send a written summary. Recording without consent is a privacy violation under GDPR and a professional misconduct.

How much does an online holistic session cost in Italy?

Prices vary widely by discipline and practitioner experience. In Italy, an individual session typically costs between 50 and 150 euros. Brief Numerology or Astrology readings start at 40-60 euros. Full ThetaHealing®, Reiki, or structured holistic counselling sessions sit in the 70-130 euro range. Multi-session packages often offer a 10-20% discount.

How do I choose among the many online holistic practitioners?

Look for three concrete things: documented training (school, years of study, certifications), a clear description of the method (what they do, what they do not do), and verified reviews. Be wary of those who promise healings or extraordinary results. A good practice is to start with a brief free introductory call (many practitioners offer one) to see whether the connection works before paying for a full session.
